concept.headline: Case classes are concise immutable data models.
concept.explanation: They auto-generate constructor, equals, toString, and copy.
concept.syntax: case class User(name: String, score: Int)

demo.intro: Create and print a case class instance.

prediction.question: Which method is auto-generated for case classes?

explanation.afterDemo: Case classes are the default data model choice in Scala.

practice.prompt: Create case class Point(x:Int,y:Int), create Point(2,3), print it.
practice.hint: Declare case class above object Main.
